blocks.content: The night before she was discharged, Ethan sat in the chair with his sketchbook and she watched him draw.
She could tell from here that he wasn't drawing anything in the room. His eyes were unfocused, turned inward. Drawing from memory.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: What are you drawing?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You. Asleep on the couch. From last Wednesday.
She looked at him.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You were drawing me from memory?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I have a very good memory.
A pause.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't want to go home and pretend.
He set the sketchbook down.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't want to tiptoe. I don't want to be something fragile you're carrying from room to room. I want to go home and be in our house and be your wife and not have this follow us everywhere.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Okay.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Promise me.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I promise.
She was quiet for a moment. Then:

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want a Christmas tree. A real one. I want to argue with you about which one.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: We'll argue about the one that's either too short or too tall.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You always want the too-tall one.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: It fills the room.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: It fills the room because it doesn't fit in the room.
He smiled. She smiled.
It was small. It was everything.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want to cook on Sundays again. I want to go to that farmers market on Saturdays. I want all the ordinary of it back. Every boring, unremarkable piece of it.
He got up from the chair and sat beside her on the edge of the bed.
She leaned her head against his shoulder.
Outside, the city at night — its lights and its indifferent forward motion. Inside, just the monitor and the two of them and tomorrow, which was almost here.
